data.narrative:
Rigetti Computing is a public-stage quantum computing company currently valued at $5.5B. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage quantum computing companies: IonQ ($11.0B), D-Wave Quantum ($2.0B), Infleqtion ($1.8B), and Archer Materials ($60M), spanning a range from $60M to $11.0B.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields an implied valuation of $1.9B. This method identifies the median across the four public quantum computing peers, offering a benchmark grounded in actual market transactions within the sector. The median estimate of $1.9B sits substantially below Rigetti's current $5.5B valuation, reflecting the wide dispersion within the public quantum computing universe—a sector characterized by material valuation volatility and heterogeneous investor expectations around technical feasibility and commercialization timelines.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.6B to $2.2B (low-to-high), centered on the $1.9B peer median. The primary uncertainty stems from limited methodological depth—only one triangulation method is currently applicable—and the extreme range embedded in the peer set itself, which spans nearly two orders of magnitude. This range suggests that peer comparables alone provide limited precision for frontier-stage quantum companies, where investor conviction varies substantially around competing technological approaches and time-to-revenue assumptions.
